title: analytics — retention windows
description: Retention is compliance-anchored. Events retain 2 years by default (5 with extended entitlement); deliveries retain 90 days (support-debugging window); identities retain 1 year post-identify per GDPR minimisation. TenantErased cascades hard-delete every table.
compliance_regimes_referenced: gdpr Art. 5(1)(e) — storage limitation ('kept in a form which permits identification for no longer than is necessary')
gdpr Art. 17 — right to erasure cascades event redaction + identity deidentification
casl (Canadian anti-spam) — consent audit trail retention

hot.description: 2 years default — supports year-over-year rollups + funnel windowing beyond a single yearly cycle.

cold.description: No cold tier — events are either hot or purged.

override.description: Enterprise + regulatory-heavy tenants extend to 5 years.

hot.description: 90 days — support-debugging window past which the provider-side dashboard is authoritative.

retention.note: Deliveries purge BEFORE their parent event (they RESTRICT event deletion).

hot.description: Retained while is_active OR while deleted_at within 90-day grace.

purge.action: hard delete + write summary row into audit

hot.description: Dangling anonymous_ids with no activity for 90 days are purged. They contribute no useful signal past that window.

purge.trigger: last_seen_at > 90 days AND identified_user_id IS NULL

hot.description: 1 year post-identified_at per GDPR Art. 5(1)(e) minimisation. Extended to match parent User lifecycle when User is still active.

purge.trigger: identified_at > 365 days AND parent User erased (deleted or GDPR-purged)

cold.description: 7y for SOC 2 CC7.3 evidence — aligned with foundation's audit retention tier.

retention.action: hard delete every table via PurgeAnalyticsDataForErasedTenantListener + write a summary audit row.
retention.note: No grace — tenant erasure is a synchronous, complete purge.
